programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.desc: Callen, Sam, Kensi and Deeks cancel their Christmas plans and jet off to East Asia to investigate the death of a fellow NCIS special agent. Meanwhile, back at headquarters, festive celebrations are getting under way as Nell and Eric make the most of the mistletoe lying around, and Hetty reveals that she is a real softie at heart. Drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at the Etihad Stadium and Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Blues ended in a 6-0 rout for the home side, with Sergio Aguero hitting his 11th top-flight hat-trick in the process. Plus, action from Arsenal v Southampton at the Emirates Stadium, Bournemouth v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at the Amex Stadium, Everton v Norwich City at Goodison Park, Watford v Burnley at Vicarage Road and West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Houston Texans v Indianapolis Colts at NRG Stadium(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working as a game warden in Cameron County, Texas, where he joins warden Mike Gonzalez to learn about the challenges he faces in this role(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a rare bicycle side car, a Victorian sewing stand and military drum(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to the Bala Autumn Sale in Snowdonia, north Wales, where veteran seller Emrys Jones is hoping to get well over £2,000 for each of his dogs - Jim and Ross - at auction(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and stage director Derek Jacobi enjoyed a relatively humble upbringing in Leytonstone, London, where his father ran a sweet shop and his mother worked in a drapery, but research into his maternal side of the family soon uncovers a link to a far more intriguing past. Discovering that his great-grandmother's name was Salome Laplain, Jacobi finds that he is descended from a wealthy French Huguenot who fled religious persecution, and who has connections to royalty on this side of the channel(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Matt Gillan puts three cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make poached eggs with hazelnut emulsion. Only one of the amateurs can join him in the final(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Mark Stacey search through the items brought to the RAF Museum in Hendon, London, and Paul Martin visits an exhibition of First World War flying pioneers(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their knowledge in a series of games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two

programme.desc: Musician pals Emeli Sande and Naughty Boy seek out antiques with the help of auctioneers Catherine Southon and Charlie Ross. Alon the way they discover the story of the Scottish connection at Kew Gardens and pull out all the stops on a mighty Wurlitzer cinema organ. But who will be victorious at the auction?(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses coffee to its full potential. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Tom Parker Bowles and Grace Dent. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: As the emotional impact of Hotaka's death takes its toll, the Moris set aside their conflict and hold a makeshift vigil where they make Yuto an offer - but it will mean betraying his brother. Rodney continues to deny his feelings of guilt over Tiff's death, while in Tokyo, Rei and Natsuko break Sora and Eiko out of the safe house and hit the road, with a pair of yakuza hot on their trail. Kenzo and Yuto's plans to return to Tokyo are derailed by Ian, who threatens to pass photos of them at Sarah's flat to the police(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Noel Gallagher's High Flying Birds return with music from two of the EPs they have released this year, Harry Styles makes his debut with his new single Lights Up and Tom Jones performs a Jerry Lee Lewis number, ahead of his co-hosting role on tomorrow night's show. Alabama Shakes singer and guitarist Brittany Howard has music from her debut solo LP, Jamie, musician and visual artist FKA Twigs performs and the line-up is completed by South African pianist Abdullah Ibrahim(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates travels to China and visits four very different cities. His first stop is Shenzhen, which over the past 40 years has grown from a region dominated by fishing and farmland to a bustling metropolis that is home to 12 million people and at the centre of the country's technological revolution. He meets the inventors of an earpiece that uses algorithms to translate speech into other languages, discovers more about the differing attitudes between generations, and witnesses a demonstration of a flying motorcycle(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's nearest neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists compete for the commission to create a piece for a mystery buyer in north Wales - picking up clues to the individual's style by snooping around their home(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: The friends go bowling, all dressed as their favourite TV and movie characters - Deadpool, Jon Snow, Batgirl and Captain Marvel(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: Craig's DJ career is failing and he is not himself. Only Lauren seems to sense something is up but she is doing a charity run with Yas, and has to decide which is more important(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the US Army's First Armoured Division, travelling into the desert to take part in a field exercise testing the combat readiness of troops and weaponry(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a satchel owned by a prisoner of war, a pub clock with an intriguing past and a sea-serpent pen holder. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps more people living and working in remote rural areas to find love. The first episode introduces Grace, a sheep farmer from Wales, and Martin, a dairy farmer from Yorkshire. Grace faces inheriting her father's business when he retires, so is looking for a partner who will be willing to share the responsibility. Martin has not had a relationship in more than a decade, so leaves behind his Doncaster roots to attend a speed dating event in London attended by women from across the UK who have written to the show(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Live debate and discussion of the issues that matter to voters, in a special programme from Norwich(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Street Auction
programme.desc: Paul Martin helps raise funds for a deserving member of a local community, using hidden treasures found in their street(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: Farmer John Dix sets out to sell nearly a tonne of South Lincolnshire Maris Piper potatoes, but due to a glut, the price may not be what he wishes to fetch(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Jerry Hall sets out to discover how her father's family, who she knew were originally from Oldham, ended up in the United States, and her investigation takes her from the cotton mills of Lancashire to the plains of Texas at the time of the railroad. She also explores her mother's side of the family and discovers her ancestors' pioneering roots as they headed west across the United States at the time of the Frontier, and finds out about a connection to the original pioneer and American folk hero, Daniel Boone(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Aiden Byrne, Stephen Terry, Mary Ann Gilchrist and Matt Gillan compete with their chosen home cook partners, with Pierre Koffmann judging their final dishes(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: As all the couples return from Blackpool, Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of their progress. Gethin Jones will be backstage chatting to the contestants, while Zoe and Rylan keep the sofa warm in the Two studio along with some celebrity super fans, who deliver their verdict on recent performances(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills contenders on chosen subjects the Gospel of Matthew, the novel and musical Wicked, composer Johannes Brahms and the history of Ireland during the 1920s and ‘30s. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: It's been raining for days and vet Maurice King is called into the practice car park to examine a newborn lamb that's in danger of succumbing to hypothermia. The birthing season in the Mournes is reaching its peak as Rachael Frew determines a heavily pregnant ewe must undergo an emergency C-section. Meanwhile, Flame the dachshund and Monty the fell pony present worrying symptoms that put the vets' knowledge to the test. Narrated by Kevin Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: Bernard summons Malachia to question him about the occurrences in the Abbey. Adso is bemused when he sees Salvatore with a cat in a sack and is told it is for a love potion. Remigio the cellarer is surprised when Anna arrives at the abbey and recognises her as the daughter of the heretic Dolcino and she asks him for her father's letters which he had been asked to keep safe. Severinus's body is found in his laboratory and Remigio is the prime suspect, but William and Adso have little time to search for a strange book that Severinus told them about. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to South America to work in Vira Vira, a hacienda hotel that overlooks an active volcano near Pucon, Chile(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Ed Gamble and Mark Simmons and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Comprehensive coverage of the day's national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the programme, featuring performances by rock band Noel Gallagher's High Flying Birds, American musician Brittany Howard, former One Direction member Harry Styles, singer-songwriter FKA twigs and South African pianist Abdullah Ibrahim. Plus hosts Jools and Tom come together at the piano with a rendition of a Sister Rosetta Tharpe classic(n)

programme.title: The Reluctant Fundamentalist (2012)
programme.desc: A Pakistani professor is visited by an American journalist, who wants to interview him about a recent kidnapping. The academic tells the story of his life in the US, and how he was falsely regarded as a potential terrorist in the aftermath of the September 11 attacks - an experience that drove him to radical politics. Drama based on Mohsin Hamid's novel, starring Riz Ahmed, Liev Schreiber, Kate Hudson and Kiefer Sutherland(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government announced the closure of investigations into alleged war crimes in Iraq and Afghanistan before a single soldier was prosecuted. But has there been a cover-up at the highest levels of the British military? Reporter Richard Bilton meets UK detectives who talk for the first time about how they were prevented from prosecuting soldiers suspected of serious crimes, revealing evidence that suggests the Ministry of Defence and senior officers were involved in the cover-up of torture and illegal killings(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: The gang performs an intervention to help Craig, but need help from their landlady(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: The gang makes New Year resolutions, but it remains to be seen how long they will last before they fall apart(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Susan Calman asks the question as Cardenen v St Joseph's(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: The looming final against the Wildcats is sending everyone a bit crazy(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss are joined by some of the stars of Strictly Come Dancing(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and Vinny search for a pet(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Poppy becomes Amelia's personal trainer(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood share love-themed stories from the natural world, including mayflies speed-dating and a kissing bug that loves humans(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall travels the globe on a quest to encounter the world's 60 deadliest animals in their natural habitats(n)

programme.title: Cars 2 (2011)
programme.desc: Racing car Lightning McQueen heads overseas to compete in an international grand prix, with events in Britain, Japan and Italy. He gets mixed up in international espionage when a case of mistaken identity leads to an encounter with two English spy cars searching for a powerful gadget. Pixar animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Owen Wilson and Michael Caine(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: John Cooper Clarke and Phill Jupitus embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge in East Anglia, as well as taking a look at the Norwich School of 19th-century artists(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to prepare a croque monsieur in 15 minutes for Marcus Wareing, while Monica Galetti sets three more the task of making pea risotto. The six contestants move on to the Signature Dish stage, where they must inspire both judges with their own unique creation before three are chosen to go through to the quarter final and three leave the contest(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to cook monkfish with vermouth sauce for Marcus, while three more chefs make a crepe souffle with a caramel sauce for Monica. For the second challenge, the contestants have to prepare a dish of their own design to impress the judges and earn their place in the quarter final(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Properties in St Albans, Hertfordshire, are judged by neighbours, beginning with a 1930s space-saving conversion, a Victorian terrace, and a traditional two-up, two-down(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Planner (2001)
programme.desc: A single woman who organises weddings for a living is swept off her feet by a dashing doctor, but her dreams of him becoming her husband suffer a setback when she discovers she has already been hired to organise his marriage to another woman. Romantic comedy, starring Jennifer Lopez, Matthew McConaughey, Bridgette Wilson-Sampras, Justin Chambers and Joanna Gleason(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin visit an island in Norway, spending two days in a house built on a footprint of just 100 square metres. In Spain, the pair head to a home built into a steep cliff face overlooking the Mediterranean, featuring a cantilevered terrace offering maximum sea views and a swimming pool as well as an unusual tiled roof. After viewing a house in New Zealand crafted from two separate wooden cladded structures, the duo explore a home in Canada inspired by two ships in dry dock, designed to peer over the coast, allowing the sea to pass underneath(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores Britain's connection to the fjords of Norway, with Neil Oliver visiting Bergen, which was once the country's commercial capital. Mark Horton meets shipwrights who build boats using the centuries-old methods of the Vikings, and Nick Crane discovers how the movement of ice at Geirangerfjord will one day cause a devastating tsunami(n)

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: The documentary follows throughout spring. New life begins on Skokholm off the western tip of Pembrokeshire as pufflings hatch, each of which will need to eat its own weight in fish every day. Two hermit crabs do battle on Rhossili Beach, lapwing chicks are targeted by crows after an easy meal and the eel-like sea lampreys move up into the Wye to breed(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A secret weapon goes haywire and threatens Walmington-on-Sea, so Mainwaring and his platoon abandon their cookhouse duties and speed to the rescue in Corporal Jones's van. Comedy, starring Arthur Lowe and Clive Dunn. First shown in 1972(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates a documentary looking at the habits of the species and the skills they have developed to survive in a range of environments around the world. The film explores how bears across the world have overcome the challenges of life - from finding food and raising the cubs to confronting rivals and habitat loss - all thanks to brains, brawn and a remarkable ability to adapt. Part of the Natural World strand(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane, finds out how the townsfolk of Innisfail withstand the tropical storms that strike the region and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari. At Mungalla Cattle Station, he hears how aboriginal people faced brutality at the hands of 19th-century settlers and after a singalong, he makes tracks for his end destination of Townsville - the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into the topic of Questions and Qualifications, with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Ade Adepitan, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The documentary explores how novels through the ages have embraced the class struggle, from Dickens, Gaskell and Hardy through to Robert Tressell's The Ragged-Trousered Philanthropists and the group of working-class writers who began to write their own stories in post-war Britain. With readings by Russell Tovey, Darren Boyd, Alex Macqueen, Emily Berrington, Freddie Fox, Rian Gordon and Olivia Barrowclough(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: With Blanca grieving for a sudden loss, Elektra takes it upon herself to organise an out-of-town trip for the girls. Starring MJ Rodriguez and Dominique Jackson(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: A new ball category is introduced that stirs up old traumas for Pray Tell. Blanca struggles to pick up the pieces and reassemble the troops following a health setback. US drama, starring MJ Rodriguez and Billy Porter(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines the physics that explains how the solar system was formed from a chaotic cloud of gas. He compares its shaping with a tornado sweeping through Oklahoma, investigates Saturn's rings and demonstrates the influence of that planet's 60 moons - including Enceladus, with its spears of ice that are shot kilometres into space(n)

programme.title: Bob Monkhouse: The Last Stand
programme.desc: Cameras capture the quiz show host and comedian in the summer of 2003 - just months before his death - as he entertains a room full of fellow comics with stand-up, chat and a masterclass in his craft. The night became the stuff of legend among comedians, but was never transmitted in full until recently(n)

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: Angela Thorne narrates a look back at clips of the comedian and presenter's appearances on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows, which have become classic viewing(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Sue Townsend (Aged 68 3/4)
programme.desc: Julie Walters narrates a profile of the author and creator of Adrian Mole. Sue Townsend left school at 14 with no qualifications, and was struggling to support three children when her writing career began. The critical and commercial success of the first Adrian Mole book led to her becoming one of the best-selling writers of the 1980s. Featuring contributions from friends, family, comedians and writers she inspired, and pupils at her former school, as well as entries from her own diary, documenting her struggles with ill health(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy receives a mysterious letter on Christmas Eve 1925 and realises that the Shelbys are in danger of annihilation, despite his new-found and unprecedented legitimacy. As the enemy closes in, Tommy flees his country house and returns to the only safe place he knows - Small Heath, Birmingham, where the family must find a way to put differences aside, take up arms and fight for survival(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: As the Shelbys come to terms with the shocking events of Christmas Day, Tommy tries to unite his family and makes a decision he may come to regret, enlisting the help of tough Romany Gypsy Aberama Gold, who wants something unusual in return. Jessie Eden confronts Tommy about the workers' pay, warning him that revolution is in the air. As the situation plays out, Tommy's factory manager tells him he has one more meeting, with a mysterious businessman from Paris, but what transpires is no ordinary meeting, with no ordinary businessman(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Italians launch another attack on the Peaky Blinders and Tommy realises that the Shelbys need to evolve if they are to survive, but some of the family are reluctant to part with tradition. As the strike takes hold at the Lanchester factory, Tommy pays a personal visit to Jessie Eden, but is outmanoeuvred when she reveals something she knows about his past, while Changretta plans further ways to continue the vendetta in the most devastating way possible. Elsewhere, Luca makes direct contact with someone at the heart of the Peaky Blinders organisation(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by groups and solo artists from the decade, beginning with music by the Kinks, Roxy Music, Elton John and Queen, plus a rendition of The Jean Genie by David Bowie recorded in January 1973(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Nicky Campbell present the edition first aired on November 3, featuring performances by Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Robert Palmer, Tanita Tikaram, Bryan Ferry, Robin Beck, Kylie Minogue, Yazz, Enya and Guns N' Roses(n)

programme.title: Sound of Song
programme.desc: Composer and musician Neil Brand investigates how songs were recorded for the first time, the listening revolution that followed and the crooner style of singing brought about by the arrival of the microphone. He also looks at the songwriting prowess of Irving Berlin, as well as the interpretative powers of singers Bessie Smith, Louis Armstrong and Bing Crosby(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Sybil Ruscoe present the edition first aired on November 10, featuring performances by Brother Beyond, Bryan Ferry, Robin Beck, Salt-N-Pepa, INXS, Chris de Burgh, Deacon Blue, Enya and Prince.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form and how it evolved across the 20th century. The first programme explores the genre's earliest years up to 1933, when so-called 'hillbilly music' grew in popularity. After centuries of percolating in the American South, hillbilly began reaching more people through the new technologies of phonographs and radio. The Carter Family, with their ballads and old hymns, and Jimmie Rodgers, with his combination of blues and yodeling, became its first big stars. Contributors include Dolly Parton, Rosanne Cash and Merle Haggard. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form focuses on the years 1933 to 1945, when the country music industry evolved with Nashville as its heart. The genre grew in popularity during the Great Depression and the Second World War, when America fell in love with singing cowboys, Texas Swing and the Grand Ole Opry's Roy Acuff. Contributors include Emmylou Harris, Vince Gill, Marty Stuart, Ray Benson, Merle Haggard, Willie Nelson and Ricky Skaggs. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Music Awards 2019
programme.desc: Highlights of the CMA Awards, which took place on November 13, with hosts Carrie Underwood, Dolly Parton and Reba McEntire. Awarded annually by the Country Music Association, they are the genre's equivalents of the Oscars or the Grammys - the biggest, most prestigious awards of the year. Featuring performances by artists including Dolly Parton, Pink and Chris Stapleton, Keith Urban, Miranda Lambert and Maren Morris(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Shelbys are lured by the Italians into a cat-and-mouse chase on the street of Birmingham, where it becomes clear that Tommy has met his match. Tommy tries to console himself with a visit from an old flame, but it soon becomes clear that he cannot always get what he wants. As his factory lies idle, Tommy confronts the possibility that the Communists might win and he'll be deemed a traitor to his class. Meanwhile, Changretta prepares to spring another trap. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy finds himself engaged in bloody battle with Luca Changretta and his gang. The family gather to find out what happened, but Lizzie has even greater news to break. Meanwhile, an army colonel has questions for Ada about her past as a Communist, and Jessie Eden confirms just how far she is prepared to go in pursuit of her cause. Sensing an opportunity to capitalise on his situation, Luca Changretta makes his way to London to present a plan to Alfie Solomons. Starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Adrien Brody(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The night of the big fight arrives, and as the bell rings and the crowd goes wild, dangers lurk in the shadows for Tommy Shelby and his family, and Tommy soon discovers who he can truly trust. Meanwhile, as Changretta plays his final ace, he sets in motion a series of events that will change the Peaky Blinders forever. programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker and Angellica Bell(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben's conscience kicks in when he realises how much Martin hates working for him, Gray tells Chantelle he is scared she will leave him and Max is humiliated when a customer at the club assumes he is Ruby's dad. Karen railroads Billy into giving her a job at the E20 and Jack steps in when a situation gets out of hand in a courtroom, oblivious to the identity of the man he has arrested(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: The daily lives of two horse vets working from their clinic in Carmarthenshire. Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes face an uncooperative wild Welsh mountain pony stallion who needs sedating before an operation, an elderly horse with a wobbly tooth and a champion mare with a badly gashed leg facing emergency treatment(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Samantha Buca is reduced to tears when her best friend visits the shop to ask her a special question. She also helps more customers find the perfect dress, including bridesmaid Linzi, who wants a frock that's bigger than the bride's, and lovebirds Daniella and Shane, who are bucking tradition by visiting the shop together(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Danny Dyer wants to know more about his tough working-class family in the East End of London and hopes his history will 'freak a few people out'. But he gets more than he bargained for when he unearths an extraordinary lineage stretching back to the 11th century(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Bolton, Greater Manchester. The panellists are Conservative Housing Secretary Robert Jenrick, Labour Shadow Lord Chancellor Richard Burgon, Liberal Democrat Foreign Affairs spokesperson Chuka Umunna, SNP health spokesperson Phillipa Whitford and Daily Telegraph columnist Sherelle Jacobs(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the process to leave the EU - and no doubt the forthcoming General Election - with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a flat in Folkestone, Kent and a house in Earby, Lancashire, as well as a revisit to a West Midlands commercial premises first featured in 2016(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A jewellery lover with form loses her sparkle when the companies she targets identify multiple scams, while a hungry hotel guest outstays his welcome by faking an injury. Disaster on the dance floor ends in court with a twinkle-toed claimant in a tangle when CCTV reveals all, and a holidaymaker tries it on with phone scam that just won't fly as records highlight his handset is still up and running. Meanwhile, a chancer staging a car crash fails as he catches himself out with in-car technology. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police aim to put a stop to the drug supply in Derby, and take away any assets and cash that have been made illegally. The RNLI are called out to assist a yacht in distress - but end up alerting Border Force after it refuses to be rescued. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a house-hunting mission with a dynamic couple in the Herefordshire countryside, hoping they fall in love with the homes they see for their £565,000 budget. programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur Sarah Moore salvages three skip-bound items from the recycling centre in Witley. To help her give them a new lease of life, Bristol Upholstery Collective gets busy with a box of patchwork pieces, and furniture restyler Emma Walker is challenged with putting her stamp on a dull dressing table. Sarah gives new life to a doll's house in her own project, but will she be able to find new homes for all her items?(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is thrilled by Ben's new positive approach to work, and Martin heads to the Slaters but is shocked by what he finds. programme.desc: Comedian David Mitchell assumes the role of host, as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Stand-up comedian Maisie Adam and former MP Rory Stewart join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by racing star Lewis Hamilton, fresh from winning his sixth Formula One world title, and actress Elizabeth Banks, who is co-starring as Susan Bosley in the new Charlie's Angels movie, which she also wrote and directed. Ricky Gervais discusses the world tour of his new stage show, SuperNature, and pop queen Kylie Minogue chats and performs a special version of her hit single Slow and another bunch of intrepid audience members tries to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Elaine attends a business networking event for powerful women and bumps into old friend Meinir, owner of the Organic Wellness Centre in Swansea. Meanwhile, the team promotes healthy living, with Wyn making the case for cycling in Wales as he dons lycra to showcase his pedalling prowess(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Miss Cardiff finalist Sophie, who often tries to replicate the looks of celebrities, takes up the challenge of make-up-free dates with five men who have been matched to her. Stylist Nathan and her fellow beauty queens are on hand to provide moral support(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Return of the comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, following a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living. Fleabag attends an uncomfortable family dinner alongside her sister Claire and brother-in-law Martin, to celebrate her godmother and father's engagement, where she finds herself intrigued by a priest. The evening comes to a tempestuous end however, when an unexpected attack brings old tensions bubbling to the surface. With Bill Paterson, Olivia Colman and Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the second episode, brother and sister Simon and Sarah from Bristol, friends Rita and Angela from London, and grandson and grandad Alex and Steve from Port Talbot in Wales take part(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The truth about Lev's past makes Ruby feel uncomfortable. programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at the Etihad Stadium and Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Blues ended in a 6-0 rout for the home side, with Sergio Aguero hitting his 11th top-flight hat-trick in the process. Plus, action from Arsenal v Southampton at the Emirates Stadium, Bournemouth v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at the Amex Stadium, Everton v Norwich City at Goodison Park, Watford v Burnley at Vicarage Road and West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.desc: In the eighth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to host a corporate away day onboard the iconic Belmond British Pullman Train, arranging food, drinks and entertainment and also securing a good ticket price. programme.desc: A car scam backfires badly when investigators spot the tell-tale signs and a fraudster claims for a damaged watch that is flagged as being stolen. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Wales track down crime gangs selling drugs out of suburban homes, and a luxury yacht, once a criminal's pride and joy, is now up for sale at a proceeds-of-crime auction(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and Nick Hall help two teams scour Nottingham racecourse for items, while presenter Charlie Ross tests people's knowledge of hallmarks(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The new practice manager starts work amid much confusion. Jimmi searches for a new car and Ruhma deals with a woman whose superstitions are troubling her in the build-up to a birth(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads on a house hunting mission in rural Devon for a couple who are looking for their first home together, with a budget of £525,000(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to Neil Wragg to come up with a creative new use for an old kitesurfing sail, while the Bristol Upholstery Collective is tasked with turning a forlorn futon into something fabulous. Sarah also takes on the ambitious project of transforming a wine bottle into lighting(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a satchel owned by a prisoner of war, a pub clock with an intriguing past and a sea-serpent pen holder. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Northern Ireland Conservative Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker and Angellica Bell(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben's conscience kicks in when he realises how much Martin hates working for him, Gray tells Chantelle he is scared she will leave him and Max is humiliated when a customer at the club assumes he is Ruby's dad. Karen railroads Billy into giving her a job at the E20 and Jack steps in when a situation gets out of hand in a courtroom, oblivious to the identity of the man he has arrested(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Danny Dyer wants to know more about his tough working-class family in the East End of London and hopes his history will 'freak a few people out'. But he gets more than he bargained for when he unearths an extraordinary lineage stretching back to the 11th century(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Bolton, Greater Manchester. The panellists are Conservative Housing Secretary Robert Jenrick, Labour Shadow Lord Chancellor Richard Burgon, Liberal Democrat Foreign Affairs spokesperson Chuka Umunna, SNP health spokesperson Phillipa Whitford and Daily Telegraph columnist Sherelle Jacobs(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the process to leave the EU - and no doubt the forthcoming General Election - with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a flat in Folkestone, Kent and a house in Earby, Lancashire, as well as a revisit to a West Midlands commercial premises first featured in 2016(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A jewellery lover with form loses her sparkle when the companies she targets identify multiple scams, while a hungry hotel guest outstays his welcome by faking an injury. Disaster on the dance floor ends in court with a twinkle-toed claimant in a tangle when CCTV reveals all, and a holidaymaker tries it on with phone scam that just won't fly as records highlight his handset is still up and running. Meanwhile, a chancer staging a car crash fails as he catches himself out with in-car technology. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police aim to put a stop to the drug supply in Derby, and take away any assets and cash that have been made illegally. The RNLI are called out to assist a yacht in distress - but end up alerting Border Force after it refuses to be rescued. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Sandown Park racecourse, where experts Raj Bisram and Catherine Southon help two teams acquire items of value to sell on for a profit. Before the excitement of the auction, Eric meets a group of students and their treasured vintage fire engine, Jezebel(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear starts feeling the pressure of the job, while Gareth has a suggestion for Al. Rob investigates a murder, and Emma throws some new light on the investigation(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory is forced to turn to the sleuthing priest for help when a senior member of the police force is murdered at a bowls match. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a house-hunting mission with a dynamic couple in the Herefordshire countryside, hoping they fall in love with the homes they see for their £565,000 budget. Along the way, the presenter heads to the banks of the River Wye to gets to grips with paddle boarding, an ever growing low-impact water sport(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur Sarah Moore salvages three skip-bound items from the recycling centre in Witley. To help her give them a new lease of life, Bristol Upholstery Collective gets busy with a box of patchwork pieces, and furniture restyler Emma Walker is challenged with putting her stamp on a dull dressing table. Sarah gives new life to a doll's house in her own project, but will she be able to find new homes for all her items?(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces debate from Sheffield, with Leader of the Labour Party Jeremy Corbyn, First Minister of Scotland and Leader of the Scottish National Party Nicola Sturgeon MSP, Leader of the Liberal Democrats Jo Swinson and Prime Minister and Leader of the Conservative Party Boris Johnson facing topical questions from an audience. All four of the participants will be keen to impress the voting public ahead of next month's General Election - the first to take place during December since 1923(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is thrilled by Ben's new positive approach to work, and Martin heads to the Slaters but is shocked by what he finds. Max plans to prove to Ruby he is serious about her by proposing, while newlyweds Tiffany and Keegan's dinner party does not go to plan. Karen starts her trial at the E20, where Billy begins to see her in a new light(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian David Mitchell assumes the role of host, as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Stand-up comedian Maisie Adam and former MP Rory Stewart join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: From the city of Derry-Londonderry, American comedian Des Bishop joins regular jokesters Neil Delamere, Jake O'Kane, Colin Murphy and Tim McGarry to poke fun at the people and events which have been making the news at home and across the planet over the past week(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by racing star Lewis Hamilton, fresh from winning his sixth Formula One world title, and actress Elizabeth Banks, who is co-starring as Susan Bosley in the new Charlie's Angels movie, which she also wrote and directed. Ricky Gervais discusses the world tour of his new stage show, SuperNature, and pop queen Kylie Minogue chats and performs a special version of her hit single Slow and another bunch of intrepid audience members tries to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Return of the comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, following a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living. Fleabag attends an uncomfortable family dinner alongside her sister Claire and brother-in-law Martin, to celebrate her godmother and father's engagement, where she finds herself intrigued by a priest. The evening comes to a tempestuous end however, when an unexpected attack brings old tensions bubbling to the surface. With Bill Paterson, Olivia Colman and Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the eighth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to host a corporate away day onboard the iconic Belmond British Pullman Train, arranging food, drinks and entertainment and also securing a good ticket price. The task will require the teams to show off their organisation and hospitality skills as they hope to impress the corporate clients. For one team the journey gets off to a bumpy start, while the others are left red faced when they have to deal with hungry guests. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Rick Stein and Alison Roman, and special guest Ben Miller. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Colleagues Ali, Noura, Davie and Ewan from the Edinburgh branch of the Cyrenians charity compete against one another in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v Chelsea in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the British Motor Museum in Gaydon, Warwickshire, where experts Richard Madley and Thomas Plant help two teams pick up items to sell on at auction for the highest profit possible. Prior to heading to the auction in Lichfield, Staffordshire, Charlie learns about the military history of Cannock Chase(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees what people are about to throw away at Dunbar Recycling Centre, rescuing a set of drawers, an unwanted computer desk and an old accordion case for transformation(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher and Kirsten Ramsay restore an art deco lamp, Will Kirk tackles a wooden sea chest and Matthew Nickels completely overhauls a precious stained glass window(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special celebrity edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rachel Fairburn, Kiri Pritchard-Mclean, Tommy Cannon, Bobby Ball, Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon, Lindsey Russell and Radzi Chinyanganya(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the second episode, brother and sister Simon and Sarah from Bristol, friends Rita and Angela from London, and grandson and grandad Alex and Steve from Port Talbot in Wales take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman return to the Elstree studio after last week's trip to Blackpool, which saw Michelle Visage and Giovanni Pernice leave the contest. As well as the usual ballroom battle, judge Bruno Toniolo will be treating the audience to a rendition of Can't Take My Eyes Off You, accompanied by the a dance number by the professionals(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian returns with his evening entertainment show, recorded in front of an audience at the London Palladium. The first edition features a musical performance by Liam Payne, who performs his latest single, Stack it Up, and survival expert Bear Grylls hands his phone over to the host for Send To All(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The truth about Lev's past makes Ruby feel uncomfortable. Meanwhile, a surprise visit from her mother at the hospital puts Rosa and David's fake relationship to the test, and after dealing with a patient who brings back traumatic memories from the terrorist attack, Ethan decides he needs to get away(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at the Etihad Stadium and Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Blues ended in a 6-0 rout for the home side, with Sergio Aguero hitting his 11th top-flight hat-trick in the process. Plus, action from Arsenal v Southampton at the Emirates Stadium, Bournemouth v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at the Amex Stadium, Everton v Norwich City at Goodison Park, Watford v Burnley at Vicarage Road and West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Houston Texans v Indianapolis Colts at NRG Stadium(n)

programme.title: Welsh Assembly
programme.desc: The day's proceedings in the Welsh Assembly including questions to the Minister for Finance and the Minister for International Relations and the Welsh Language, questions to the Assembly Commission, topical questions and debates on a workplace parking levy, hospice and palliative care and on rolling stock for railways in Wales(n)

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: As the political parties continue to try and win over voters on the election trail, an audience in Edinburgh put their questions to politicians from the larger parties in Scotland. programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Chef Gino D'Acampo tours his homeland by train, taking in some of some of the most scenic train journeys in Europe. Once in his own country, he meets a doctor who does his rounds on horseback, and visits hazelnut groves where he prepares a chocolate cheesecake. programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter explores scenic Britain on foot, beginning in north Wales where she heads out beside the Menai Straits overlooking Snowdonia. During the trek, Julia seeks out the lost prototype of the first ever Land Rover, which locals believe may be buried in the Anglesey sand-dunes where its inventor first put it through trials almost 70 years ago. The expert walker also discovers why Prince William and his wife have a soft spot for the romantic St Llanddwyn Island, home to the Welsh St Valentine. Finally, she meets mussel and oyster pickers, getting to taste their wares in a sunset beach picnic(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: As Gemma and Bernie return with the laptops and photos, Paul is horrified to see his terrible past laid before him. Elsewhere, Aggie jeopardises Michael's negotiations with Grace, and Peter and Carla worry about Daniel(n)

Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

To do so, Nigel visits three London-based women's homes and is faced with a tricky choice when it comes to picking one to take on a date. Simon Cowell mentors the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger is in charge of the Overs, and Louis Walsh handles the Unders, and as usual the judges are each allowed to send one act through to next week by pushing the golden buzzer, leaving the rest at the mercy of the voting public(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!

programme.desc: Chef Gino D'Acampo tours his homeland by train, taking in some of some of the most scenic train journeys in Europe. Once in his own country, he meets a doctor who does his rounds on horseback, and visits hazelnut groves where he prepares a chocolate cheesecake. programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter explores scenic Britain on foot, beginning in north Wales where she heads out beside the Menai Straits overlooking Snowdonia. During the trek, Julia seeks out the lost prototype of the first ever Land Rover, which locals believe may be buried in the Anglesey sand-dunes where its inventor first put it through trials almost 70 years ago. The expert walker also discovers why Prince William and his wife have a soft spot for the romantic St Llanddwyn Island, home to the Welsh St Valentine. Finally, she meets mussel and oyster pickers, getting to taste their wares in a sunset beach picnic(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa's hippie mother Eleanor (Louise Jameson) arrives in the village, immediately taking over Martin's kitchen to prepare a herbal tonic, which she later gives to the baby to stop his crying. A culinary rivalry develops between Bert and Mark Bridge, the landlord of the Crab and Lobster pub, at Portwenn Fun Day, with both keen to take advantage of the event by providing food for the spectators(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: David's exoneration allows him to regain custody of his children and intensifies the investigation into Rob, Alice and Tom, leading to a critical arrest. But as damaging accusations concerning DI Hudson's relationship with David threaten to derail the case, her determination to deliver justice finally begins to unravel the mystery of who killed Tara. Drama, starring Lee Ingleby, Hermione Norris, Daniel Ryan and Angel Coulby(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack is offered the job of bus inspector and Stan is promised extra overtime, for which he will not have to work - as well as a glamorous conductress. Reg Varney and Bob Grant star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Arthur is attracted to Beryl, the clippie on his regular bus. However, she has only two interests in life - men with a fondness for athletics, and those with a good head of hair. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When the trio decide they need a fourth person to share the flat, Robin is determined it will be a woman. Seventies comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The residents of Aidensfield are terrorised by a blackmailer and Sergeant Merton is less than impressed by the investigative methods of the handsome newcomer PC Rob Walker. Meanwhile, Vernon embarks on a venture in the prestigious world of publishing, with the help of David and Bernie. Jonathan Kerrigan stars(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Turner has some bad news for Seth, Elsa is an unwelcome visitor to Demdyke, and at the Woolpack, Pollard is reduced to drowning his sorrows(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael confronts Vic to make Scott suffer the consequences. Lynn is feeling broody, while Elizabeth is reminded that marriage is for better or for worse(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tanya looks forward to a weekend in Amsterdam with Alex, Curly interrogates 10-year-old Jamie Armstrong after Ivy catches him shoplifting, and Sally tells Kevin about the baby(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is upset as Kevin refuses to talk about the baby, Tricia makes a play for Curly, Denise finds out people are talking about Ken and her, and Derek has harsh words for Mavis(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The residents of Aidensfield are terrorised by a blackmailer and Sergeant Merton is less than impressed by the investigative methods of the handsome newcomer PC Rob Walker. Meanwhile, Vernon embarks on a venture in the prestigious world of publishing, with the help of David and Bernie. Jonathan Kerrigan stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is shocked by the unexpected arrival of his ex-wife Maggie (Julie Graham), who appears to have forgotten that they separated years ago and wants to know why he left her. Eleanor goes on a date with local fisherman Paul Hale, an old friend she has not seen since school, while Martin and Louisa finally agree on a name for their baby - James Henry. With Martin Clunes, John Marquez and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective delves into the past of extreme sports enthusiast Gideon Frane after he plunges from a cliff in a ball of flames. A scan of the files reveals a long list of stalking complaints against an ex, and an anonymous letter to the victim spelling out Burn in Hell is linked to one of her friends. Aided by DC Shepherd and the newly appointed DC Edwards, the team tries to track down Gideon's old gang to uncover a lifetime of bullying and an unexpected story of rough justice(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Bob Hoskins stars in the return of Jimmy McGovern's interlinked drama anthology. He plays pub landlord Paddy Gargan, who finds himself on the wrong side of a local gangster after barring the man's son from the pub for smoking in the toilets. With neither man willing to back down, the disagreement becomes a matter of mutual pride and self-respect - and violence seems inevitable. Also starring Liam Cunningham, Frances Barber and Timothy Spall(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Anna Friel plays a single mother who turns to prostitution to pay her debts and provide for her sons' schooling. Things seem to be going well, and when her boiler breaks, romance beckons with the arrival of a handsome plumber. However, her life turns sour when his father recognises her and warns her off his son and grand-daughter, just as she is beginning to fall in love. Also starring David Bradley and Daniel Mays(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The bus crews go on strike, causing discord at work and in the Butler household, where Arthur is particularly peeved. Very first episode of the vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan falls for attractive new clippie Iris, but Mum takes a dim view of their romance and tries to nip it in the bud. Vintage Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Young physiotherapist Lizzie Faulkner is shot dead while enjoying a holiday at a luxury country retreat. The resulting murder investigation unearths betrayal, guilt and the desire for atonement - as Vera moves quickly to avoid another tragedy, and Joe tries to make amends with the demons of his past. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Paul Ritter and Eva Quinn(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Business is bad for Steve so he has to borrow money to give Mike the rent. Des persists in trying to date Raquel, asking her to break her date with Erik(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen and Reg are devastated by Maud's move, Denise is aghast when Ken takes her to the Over 60s club for a joke, and Raquel tells Des that she would like to move in with him(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth is in Leeds to meet her daughter, Chris's suspicions prove unfounded, and Kathy makes up her mind to do the decent thing(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Turner solves all Carol's problems, while Pollard creates more for Elizabeth. At Mill Cottage, Chris takes Zoe's revelation very badly(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan indulges a whim and lifts Doreen off the ground, but strains his back in the process. Classic comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack asks Arthur's sister out to the Boxing Day social - much to her brother's displeasure. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa's hippie mother Eleanor (Louise Jameson) arrives in the village, immediately taking over Martin's kitchen to prepare a herbal tonic, which she later gives to the baby to stop his crying. A culinary rivalry develops between Bert and Mark Bridge, the landlord of the Crab and Lobster pub, at Portwenn Fun Day, with both keen to take advantage of the event by providing food for the spectators(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)
