programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A man bets his companion he can disappear without trace, and approaches the team to help him do so. Starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a people smuggling ring, focusing his inquiries on the activities of a local yacht club. When it is revealed that one of the immigrants has gone on the run while suffering from smallpox, the Saint sets out to locate him before he can spread the disease(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Several men commit suicide at the same hotel - and Theo suspects they were murdered as part of a plot to avenge a woman who died under similarly suspicious circumstances(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A woman accuses her brother-in-law of murdering his wife, even though she seemingly died of a heart attack - and it falls to Quincy to determine the truth(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: An ex-footballer persuades Arthur to buy 2,000 tickets for an England v Scotland football match. However, trouble ensues once it becomes apparent they are forgeries. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An old adversary of Doyle's stirs up rivalry between factions of political terrorist group the Organization as a diversion in order to launch a revenge attack on the CI5 operative, who he blames for the death of his brother. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to act as go-between, taking ransom money to the kidnappers of the wayward daughter of a rich film producer. However, he has a trick up his sleeve - quite literally - in the shape of a camera disguised as a wrist-watch, so even though he is blindfolded, he can later retrace his steps. Roger Moore directs and stars(n)

Crime drama, starring Jack Klugman(n)

programme.desc: Homelessness is on the rise in the UK, but nobody knows how many pregnant women face the possibility of having their baby without a home to go back to. This programme explores the issue of expectant mothers who find themselves with no place to call home, and conducts a survey of midwives in collaboration with the Royal College of Midwives to try to get a sense of the scale of the problem. Ninety-five per cent of the midwives asked strongly felt that homelessness puts the health of the women and their unborn babies at risk. The report follows three women - two of whom are heavily pregnant - experiencing evictions, overcrowding and the reality of living in temporary accommodation for years on end(n)

With risky menu choices and a fellow diner's peculiar laughter taking the spotlight, will the conversations ranging from lookalikes to riding polar bears be enough to secure Shanice the prize?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Alaska: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Autumn draws in and animals prepare for the hard winter ahead. For many young creatures, this is the point where their mothers leave them to fend for themselves. Humpback whales return to feeding grounds on the south east coast, having completed one of the longest migrations of any species, while brown bears compete for the best spots to catch salmon before their winter hibernation. Also returning to Alaska are an astonishing 300 million salmon, all looking to spawn in the very same streams in which they hatched(n)

programme.title: How to Stop Your Nuisance Calls
programme.desc: An investigation into the plague of nuisance and scam callers, such as incessant calls about PPI or accidents that never happened. Alexis Conran tells the stories of victims like Jeff, who lost £20,000 to fraudsters pretending to be his bank, and Jessica, who was brainwashed into thinking she was being monitored by the FBI. Alexis also meets police, bank representatives and members of the call centre industry to ask whether enough is being done to help, and reveals just what people can do to stop these callers(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Norway, camera follow a train journey that begins in the capital Oslo and heads all the way to Bodo inside the Arctic Circle. The first leg is the Dovre line, running from to the remote northern wonderland of Trondheim. Along the way it passes through the vast Gudbrandsdal valley and across the awe-inspiring Dovrefjell mountain plateau. The route stops at the picturesque mountain village of Dombas, before changing to the Rauma railway, which features dramatic fjords opening out along the Rauma river, dotted with some of the country's most famous landmarks. Narrated by Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Live Bellator
programme.desc: Michael Page v Derek Anderson. Coverage of the mixed martial arts event at the SSE Arena, Wembley in London, which features the welterweight contest as its main attraction. Home favourite Page suffered the first defeat of his career against current champion Douglas Lima in May, but bounced back in style to knock out Richard Kiely four months later, and enters this contest as the favourite. However, San Diego's Anderson can not be taken lightly given his solid record of 16 wins and just three defeats(n)

programme.title: Fights, Camera, Action!
programme.desc: Footage of hostile and aggressive encounters in the world of sport, including clashes between referees and fans, and fights in the stands at boxing matches(n)

programme.title: Dark Command (1940)
programme.desc: A teacher is defeated in his ambition to become the marshal of his town during the early years of the American Civil War, so he turns renegade instead. Before long he has a reputation as a feared raider fighting on the side of the South, and it falls to the newly appointed lawman whose job he coveted to stop his reign of terror. Western, starring John Wayne and Walter Pidgeon(n)

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin (1966)
programme.desc: Former crook and disreputable spy Harry Palmer has to carefully engineer the defection of a Russian officer responsible for safeguarding vital military secrets - but the mission proves less than straightforward. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deighton's character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman(n)

programme.title: Houseboat (1958)
programme.desc: An Italian socialite rebels against her domineering father and gets a job as a maid for a widowed father. While helping her employer learn to understand his three precocious children, she also falls in love with him and must decide whether or not to reveal her secret. Romantic comedy, starring Cary Grant and Sophia Loren(n)

programme.title: Arrowhead (1953)
programme.desc: A cavalry scout witnesses a series of raids by an Apache tribe that is supposedly in peace talks - but his superiors reject his story out of hand and dismiss him from duty. Continuing to spy on the tribe, he comes to suspect the chief's son is behind the attacks. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Jack Palance and Katy Jurado(n)

programme.title: Table 19 (2017)
programme.desc: Dumped by the best man via text, a former maid of honour decides to pitch up at her friend's wedding anyway. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Craig Robinson and Lisa Kudrow(n)

programme.title: Miss Sloane (2016)
programme.desc: Elizabeth Sloane is the most sought after and formidable lobbyist in Washington DC. But when taking on the most powerful opponent of her career, she finds winning may come at too high a price. Drama, starring Jessica Chastain, Mark Strong and Gugu Mbatha-Raw(n)

programme.title: A Million Ways to Die in the West (2014)
programme.desc: A timid farmer abandoned by his girlfriend is fascinated by a mysterious woman who rides into his frontier town. He tries to act the part of a gunslinging Wild West hero to impress her, only to find himself facing a showdown with her notorious outlaw husband. Western spoof, directed by and starring Seth MacFarlane, with Charlize Theron and Liam Neeson(n)

programme.title: Dracula Untold (2014)
programme.desc: A Romanian knight fights to defend his homeland against Turkish invaders. Faced with overwhelming odds, he enters into a dangerous bargain to gain supernatural powers so he can drive off his enemies - but the deal could cost him his humanity as he transforms into a terrifying monster. Fantasy adventure, starring Luke Evans, Dominic Cooper and Charles Dance(n)

programme.title: The Buccaneer (1958)
programme.desc: A general charged with the defence of New Orleans in 1812 learns the British are sending a vast fleet to retake the city. His only hope is to form an alliance with a notorious pirate - but while the buccaneer's loyalties are to his homeland, his crew urges him to side with the invaders. Remake of Cecil B DeMille's classic swashbuckling adventure, directed by Anthony Quinn, starring Yul Brynner, Charlton Heston, Claire Bloom and Charles Boyer(n)

programme.title: Tulsa (1949)
programme.desc: A cattle magnate's ambitious daughter alienates her friends with her single-minded pursuit of wealth as she attempts to fend off the unwanted attentions of greedy oil barons intent on stealing her land. Drama, starring Susan Hayward, Robert Preston, Pedro Armendariz and Lloyd Gough(n)

programme.title: Dakota (1945)
programme.desc: A professional gambler and a railway tycoon's daughter elope, hoping to make their fortune by buying land in Dakota. However, they instead find themselves helping the local farmers defend their homes against unscrupulous businessmen trying to drive them off their land. Western, starring John Wayne and Vera Hruba Ralston(n)

programme.title: Three Violent People (1956)
programme.desc: A tough rancher falls for a woman he encounters at the end of the American Civil War, and promptly sweeps her off her feet. However, the couple's attempts to settle into married life are threatened by dark secrets in her past, as well as rising tensions both within the family, and on the political scene. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Anne Baxter, Gilbert Roland and Tom Tryon(n)

programme.title: Last Holiday (2006)
programme.desc: A woman is told she has only weeks to live. Resolving to make the most of the time she has left, she heads for a French hotel presided over by her hero, a world-renowned chef, where her new-found passion for life has a remarkable effect on the other guests. Comedy, starring Queen Latifah, Gerard Depardieu, LL Cool J, Timothy Hutton and Giancarlo Esposito(n)

programme.title: Shooter (2007)
programme.desc: A former Marine sniper is coaxed back into action when government officials need his help to prevent an assassination attempt on the US president. However, the intended target is in fact an African archbishop, and the marksman is set up by double-crossing agents to take the fall, forcing him to go on the run as he fights to clear his name. Action thriller, with Mark Wahlberg, Michael Pena and Danny Glover(n)

programme.title: Don't Breathe (2016)
programme.desc: Three thieves break into the house of a blind Gulf War veteran, who is not as helpless as he seems and is also harbouring a dark secret. Horror, starring Stephen Lang, Jane Levy and Dylan Minnette(n)

programme.title: Die Hard 2 (1990)
programme.desc: Tough cop John McClane arrives at an airport to pick up his wife, but finds himself plunged into a bloody life-or-death struggle. He must battle terrorists who have seized control of the terminal as part of a campaign to secure the release of a convicted drug baron. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Bonnie Bedelia, William Sadler, Franco Nero and William Atherton(n)

programme.title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid: Dog Days (2012)
programme.desc: Weedy youngster Greg finds his plans for a lazy school holiday ruined when his mother bans video games and his father organises a series of family activities. When he finds out a girl he likes is working at a country club, he tells his family he has a summer job there as an excuse to spend time with her - but struggles to keep up the deception. Family comedy sequel, starring Zachary Gordon and Steve Zahn(n)

programme.title: Angels Sing (2013)
programme.desc: A man is forced to reassess his attitude to Christmas after meeting a cheerful, white-bearded, truck-driving stranger. Family comedy, starring Harry Connick Jr, Connie Britton, Willie Nelson and Chandler Canterbury(n)

programme.title: Epic (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager wanders into a forest that her scientist father believes is home to a race of miniature people. She stumbles on the tiny creatures and is shrunk down to their size so she can carry out a quest to save their new queen from an invading army. Animated adventure, with the voices of Amanda Seyfried, Josh Hutcherson, Colin Farrell and Christoph Waltz(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)

The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover(n)

programme.title: Non-Stop (2014)

Action thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Julianne Moore and Lupita Nyong'o(n)

programme.title: Dirty Grandpa (2016)

However, the old man is convinced his grandson is about to make a terrible mistake, and turns the road trip into a wildly hedonistic weekend in the hope of inspiring him to get more fun out of life. Comedy, starring Robert De Niro and Zac Efron(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Mother-and-daughter team Eileen and Jannika Hopeakoski invite guests to their B&B Sea-La-Vie in Southsea, Portsmouth, for the third visit of the week, and while the guests think the stylish rooms are a fine vintage, host Eileen gets hot under the collar after discovering Ian and Maggie have been trying to strip back the mattress cover(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Craig and Marie Harris welcome their guests to the Peaks Inn in Derbyshire, but a few of the guests are far from impressed upon arrival. When the hosts reveal ferret racing is their chosen activity, a joke causes Eileen to lose her temper, so Ian and Maggie decide to stage a silent protest(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hoteliers meet one last time for feedback, during which they will discover how much their rivals thought their service was worth, and which of them was the most successful. Maggie and Ian Dickson decide to tackle complaints from Eileen and Jannika Hopeakoski head-on, while Craig and Marie Harris prepare for a fight after telling German competitor Kristin Grantham her venue and welcome lacked some much-needed warmth(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carpenter Wes Leaver hosts the first party in east Kent, hoping his science theme will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize at the end of the week. However, the 34-year-old faces competition from estate agent Alexandra Austin, beautician Emma Phillips, dog-lover Sally Parry and fancy-dress enthusiast Stephen Drew(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Alexandra Austen hosts the second party of the week in east Kent, hoping to impress her guests with pan-European cuisine. However, the meal gets off to a shaky start when Sally Parry is forced to confront her fear of beans, while Stephen Drew struggles with a goat's cheese parcel. Alexandra's decision to serve veal also proves controversial, and it soon becomes clear two of the guests are not enjoying each other's company(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beautician Emma Phillips hosts the third dinner party in east Kent. Her main course is described by one diner as a pet on a plate, and Alexandra Austin declares the menu to be rather odd. Meanwhile, Stephen Drew and Wes Leaver enjoy an impromptu beauty treatment together(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-lover Sally Parry hosts the fourth dinner party in east Kent, hoping to impress her guests with a pink-themed night, including posh pink food and table decorations. However, Sally and Emma have a heated argument, Alex misbehaves and Wes falls asleep(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant Stephen Drew hosts the final dinner party in east Kent, and has designed a menu that will allow him to spend time entertaining his guests. With an Absolutely Fabulous theme, a few of the diners are expecting a Mardi Gras vibe, but he has different ideas for the night - before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant giraffe Orla goes into labour, but the keepers must watch from the sidelines due to the risks involved during the birth. Baby Bornean orang-utan Tombol tries his luck with the gibbons as he heads out and explores his new habitat. Elsewhere, northern bald ibis chick Professor X is proving too confident for his own good, and when it is time to release him into the adult habitat he gets into troubled waters and suffers a nasty head injury, so the vets have to work hard to help him pull through(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Steph is trapped with multiple injuries after a bad smash near Alnwick in Northumberland, while an accident with a bonfire leaves Paul with serious burns. Meanwhile, in Thetford Forest in Norfolk, the medics have difficulty reaching horse-rider Alice, who has badly broken her ankle(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics Jas Panesar and Amy Cullen rush to a woman who has had a severe allergic reaction at a restaurant. In Birmingham, paramedics Naomi Whatley and Mark McDonagh attend to a man who has fallen unconscious after a severe diabetic attac, while in Warwick, paramedics treat a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter(n)

programme.title: The Team
programme.desc: Nelly finds Helle and Malu in the marshland and Malu is rushed to hospital with serious injuries. Morten boasts on social media about the Marsh Farm murders and announces another strike. Karim is located in Belgium in hiding with two Caliphate Union sleepers and several crates of arms. The fourth section of the frieze is stolen from International Council of Museums staff and Malu is placed in grave peril from an attack by an unknown man at the hospital. Crime thriller, with Jurgen Vogel. In English, German, Flemish, French and Danish(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The air ambulance is dispatched to collect Frances, an 80-year-old woman who fell three metres into her empty swimming pool while gardening, and has sustained a head wound that could have resulted in a life-altering bleed on the brain. Ruth, aged 86, arrives at St George's A&E with her heart beating three times faster than the normal rate. Doctors are concerned it may not be pumping enough blood around her body, and quickly prescribe fast-acting medication. Plus, students Jerry and Sakille turn up at the hospital after Jerry crashed her car on the way home from a party. While Jerry stayed sober to serve as the designated driver, she concludes that balancing a full-time job with her studies and a hectic social life might not be a sensible idea(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 84-year-old is rushed into hospital after losing control of his car and crashing into a road sign, and is in need of an emergency transfusion after his blood pressure drops dangerously low. Elsewhere, seven-year-old Reggie needs surgery to fix his broken arm, and 66-year-old Christine comes into A&E with worrying symptoms after the entire left side of her body collapses(n)

programme.title: Congo (1995)
programme.desc: An expedition to find King Solomon's diamond mines in the jungles of the Congo disappears without trace. The tycoon behind the mission sends his assistant, a former CIA agent, to find out what became of the explorers - only to discover that the party met a grisly fate at the hands of a horde of vicious apes. Action adventure based on Michael Crichton's novel, with Dylan Walsh, Laura Linney, Tim Curry and Grant Heslov(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the team's favourite investigations. Jimmy Doherty visits Thailand to find out if the alarming headlines about additive MSG are justified, Kate Quilton heads to California to find out why there are so many closed pistachios in the packs on supermarket shelves, and Matt Tebbutt learns why slicing onions makes people cry with help from an innovative farmer and a team of cage fighters(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Ruth Jobey wants to escape from her busy London life to the relaxed town of Oliva on Spain's Costa del Azahar, or 'orange blossom coast'. She wants a detached villa with a swimming pool and views, so Ben Hillman shows her some properties for her £150,000 budget that meet that criteria(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps good friends Mike Dawson and Louise Clark search for a holiday apartment in the resort of Sitges, near Barcelona, on a budget of £170,000. Louise is hoping for somewhere to escape with her two-year-old daughter, while Mike is looking to make the first step in a potentially permanent move(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Five Bells near Dover, where manager Shelley Parnell and her competitive mum Linda Yates hope their bargain-priced B&B will win the day(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts check in to the Thomas Paine Hotel in Thetford, where an inappropriate book is found in a bedside drawer and competition turns fierce at a Home Guard training exercise(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris and Sarah Whitehead invite their fellow hoteliers to Tenbury Wells in Worcestershire, where they open the doors of their historic pub The Rose and Crown(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lesley and Steve Hawker welcome the contestants to the Woodlands Guest House in Exmoor, but after the bad feedback of previous rounds, it seems their rivals are out for revenge(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Chris and Sarah from The Rose and Crown want to question Gez, and Steve and Lesley from Woodlands Guest House try to get to the naked truth(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hotel duty manager Andrew Carruthers hosts the first dinner party from Cumbria, where he hopes to impress his fellow diners with his hotel-themed evening, complete with five-star quality food including 'early reservation fishcakes' and a 'do not disturb truffle' in an attempt to win the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Jonny Thompson hosts the second night in Cumbria, where he hopes to wow his guests with a meaty menu - and they get to see a lot more of him than they had bargained for(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eccentric sheepdog trainer Katy Cropper hosts the third dinner party in Cumbria, where she prepares 'come a cropper' prawns al fresco, opens a bottle of Champagne with a Masai sword, and serves some potent home-made horseradish sauce. Aside from the food, a sheep-herding demonstration proves to be a hit with her guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Wendy Tinnion helms the final event in Cumbria and decides to throw a Friday night disco for her fellow diners. However, her guests are in for a shock when they discover something unexpected about their host(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel transforms the attic into a studio and storage space for her vintage treasures and takes Dorothy on a trip to Paris, while Dick has his own plans for repurposing some ancient tools he finds in the outbuildings. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A freak car accident in Cumbria leaves a man fighting for his life, while in Hampshire, horse rider Pat takes a nasty fall when her animal is spooked(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 61-year-old woman is airlifted to hospital after a car mounted the pavement and ran her over and doctors quickly get her into the CT scanner to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Comedians Phill Jupitus and Marcus Brigstocke head to Bolivia for a high-altitude trip, first driving along the infamous 'Road of Death', only to find themselves deep in bandit country. Disorientation takes them by surprise as they have to navigate Salar de Uyuni - a salt lake so vast that Buzz Aldrin could see it from space. Their goal is the ancient mountain city of Potosi, whose silver mines financed the Spanish empire in the 16th century and which is almost 5,000 metres above sea level(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio of motoring enthusiasts embark on another epic road trip, this time across western America in front-engined supercars. Jeremy Clarkson pilots the Lexus LFA, which has a spectacularly high-revving V10 powerplant, Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the muscular Dodge Viper, and James May opts for the latest incarnation of Aston Martin's Vanquish. On their journey from Las Vegas to Palm Springs via Los Angeles, the presenters tackle racing circuits and find themselves in a race against the police, before rushing to the Mexican border - and there's a terrifying penalty for the last man to arrive. Meanwhile, Fleetwood Mac drummer Mick Fleetwood rolls up his sleeves to thrash the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.desc: Mike and Frank look back on their trickiest excavations ever, including relics buried underground and awesome finds trapped under mountains of junk(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike tries to put Frank in a Christmas mood with two trips in search of precious objects(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of kayakers head to the Himalayas to visit the last great unexplored river in Bhutan. Legend has it that at some point, the river drops off the face of the planet(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: CS gas is used to stop a crazed axeman, a drunk student cannot understand why he's being arrested and a joker insults a cop but uses his charm to avoid arrest(n)

programme.title: Mega Air
programme.desc: The world's biggest air package handling facility at UPS Worldport, which sorts two million items per night for worldwide delivery. Fireman also tackle a mock air freighter inferno(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets out to discover if driving a Lamborghini Murcielago roadster gives drivers the same kick as 12 angry bulls, and whether the Top Gear theme tune can be played with engines. The Ford F150 SVT Lightning pick-up is put through its paces, the world's greatest power sliders hit the track, and Timothy Spall is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond visits Abu Dhabi's Yas Marina circuit to drive the Porsche 918 Spyder, a four-wheel-drive hybrid hypercar capable of astonishing speeds thanks to the 886 horsepower it can generate from its screaming V8 petrol engine and electric motors. Jeremy Clarkson and James May join forces to create a public information film to promote safer cycling, with their efforts presented to a panel of experts including members of Westminster Council and Olympic gold medal-winning rider Chris Boardman. Meanwhile, Jeremy - who has promised to change his name to Jennifer if the Porsche is faster around the test track than the McLaren P1 driven earlier in the series - compares the seventh-generation Volkswagen Golf GTI to BMW's critically acclaimed M135i. Breaking Bad star Aaron Paul fires up the Reasonably Priced Car to see what lap time he can achieve(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: In Houston, Eddie faces the 'grim reaper' in a crawfish challenge featuring the fearsome reaper pepper. He also devours some southern comfort food and pulls a plane down a runway(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie Hall follows in Rocky's footsteps with physical challenges at a famed Philadelphia boxing gym - and his very own hunger games in the infamous Philly Cheesesteak Challenge(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Guests Fern Britton, Stephen Mangan, Ken Livingstone and Reginald D Hunter join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show in which contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon presents(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Jimmy Carr and Marcus Brigstocke, TV presenter Terry Christian and singer Jamelia join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Jack Whitehall and Frankie Boyle, TV presenter Christine Bleakley and journalist Kelvin MacKenzie join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy arranges a special surprise for Lee's birthday, which sees their family and friends joining them in a Crystal Maze-style physical adventure game where the objective is to escape from a series of rooms. Domestic comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A missing toy keyring leads to a rift between Lee and Lucy and their best friends and former neighbours Toby and Anna. Domestic comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy get a family pet in an attempt to teach the children to be more responsible. But as ever, things do not quite work out as planned. Domestic comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance from former EastEnders actress Annette Badland (Aunt Babe)(n)

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Terms and Conditions Apply
programme.desc: Using his trademark, powerpoint-comedy and a series of mischievous games, Dave invites three of the country's finest comic minds to help him make sense of our insensible world. In the first episode he is joined by Richard Osman, Phil Wang and Sara Barron, as they take a deep dive into the details we usually miss(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Carl Donnelly, Jo Caulfield and Milton Jones join host Dara O Briain and panellists Hugh Dennis, Andy Parsons and Chris Addison for another round of the satirical news quiz from June 2012(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Marcus Brigstocke, Gary Delaney and Zoe Lyons join host Dara O Briain and panellists Hugh Dennis, Andy Parsons and Chris Addison for another round of the satirical news quiz from July 2012(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The comedian makes five train journeys taking in the spectacular sights of Africa, travelling to unexpected places in deserts and forests, villages and cities, and finding out how the railways are used today. On the fourth leg of his journey across the continent, Griff travels across Namibia in the south-west, experiencing luxury camping, encountering cheetahs, and visiting 1,000-foot sand dunes(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and his team head to an army training ground on Salisbury Plain to discover what lies beneath the Barrow Clump, a huge mound of earth built by Bronze Age people to bury their dead. More than 2,500 years later, the Anglo-Saxons re-used the location as a burial site, leaving behind some fascinating artefacts. The team is joined for the dig by a group of wounded soldiers whose members are being trained in archaeology as part of their rehabilitation(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The events of 1944, when the death toll rose substantially. By this point, the Allies were aware of Auschwitz's purpose, and faced a difficult decision over what should be done about it. Plus, the story of the occupation of Hungary, revealing how Adolf Eichmann offered to spare a million Jews in return for certain services(n)

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The decade of propaganda, impassioned speeches, newspaper articles, posters and campaigning that all led to one moment - Hitler being made Chancellor of Germany in January 1933(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The US Navy's fastest combat ships, which are capable of a turn of speed only possible due to a revolutionary water propulsion system and the chance discovery of a brand new alloy(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A little-known dark past explains the vast monolithic structures that dominate the small British island of Guernsey(n)

programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough highlights the curiosities that have led to accusations of forgery, but have ultimately helped assist the rethinking of evolution. When early explorers brought the first specimen of a duck-billed platypus to England in 1799, it was considered so bizarre it was deemed a hoax. Similarly, the midwife toad became the centre of a scientific storm in the 1920s that led to accusations of fakery(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the comedian crosses 1,000 miles of South Africa, reaching the most southerly passenger railway station on the continent. Heading to Johannesburg, he reveals how the city has benefited from the scale of gold-mining in the area. Later on, he takes to the skies in a light aircraft for a bird's-eye view of one of South Africa's largest ostrich farms, and catches one of the world's most luxurious trains for a seven-hour ride to Cape Town, before reaching the coastal settlement of Simon's Town(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team heads to the small county of Rutland to visit Oakham Castle, the most well-preserved 12th-century building in Britain. It was once home to the Norman knight Walkelin de Ferrers, who fought alongside Richard the Lionheart in the Crusades, and before that the residence of several Saxon kings and queens. Tony and the crew investigate de Ferrers' long-lost private quarters, but it takes three days of twists and turns to determine what the site looked like 900 years ago. To mark the visit, they also cast a giant Time Team horseshoe to add to the collection that has decorated the castle hall for half a millennium(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The final episode examines the liberation of Auschwitz and other camps by the Soviet Red Army. However, freedom did not mean an end to the prisoners' suffering - many were mistreated by their liberators and thousands died for the want of medical treatment(n)

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: As Hitler's ambitions spread beyond Germany, the world drew toward another great war. This one would be fought on many battlefields, but also for the hearts and minds of the people(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Portugal to meet leading experts on the Three Secrets of Fatima - a series of visions and prophecies allegedly given by the Virgin Mary to three young shepherds in 1917(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to France to explore the possibility that Jesus married Mary Magdalene, and they had children. He meets respected scholars, authors and historians to consider the theory the son of God's bloodline lives on today(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Following the epic journey of a humpback whale and its calf as they travel from a tropical paradise to the species' great feeding ground in the icy polar seas. David Attenborough also observes footage of colonies of seabirds in the Arabian desert and surfing dolphins in Australia that swim onto the beach to catch their fish(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An exploration of the survival techniques used by creatures inhabiting the deep oceans of the world, where adaptation is paramount for those dwelling on the surface as well as below. Pods of dolphins are filmed charging in search of prey, surprising methods for conserving energy are revealed as deep-sea octopuses 'fly' with wings, and vampire squid use bioluminescence to create a colourful display(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica(n)

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from(n)

programme.desc: Performances by punk and new wave acts on the music show in 1977, including the Stranglers and the Jam, plus the continuing power of disco exemplified by Donna Summer's I Feel Love, the reggae of Bob Marley and pub rock by acts such as Eddie and the Hot Rods. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke helps unlock the potential of two houses in south London. He begins by helping NHS theatre nurse Hannah, whose Edwardian worker's cottage in Thornton Heath is so small she has had to give the only decent bedroom to her two children while she sleeps in the front room. Can he provide the luxurious master bedroom she needs? George also helps a couple renovate their Streatham home, which would not be so bad if not for the previous owners' love of glass chandeliers, Roman columns and marble-effect tiles(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The start of the wedding season sees Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree at their busiest, with events booked all through the summer, but there is still time to make improvements. Angel has her sights set on transforming the gravelled area at the front of the chateau into a thing of beauty and the whole family take a trip to Monet's stunning house and garden, where a plan for the chateau's very own water lily patch is hatched(n)

programme.title: Boeing's Killer Plane: What Went Wrong?
programme.desc: Unravelling the events that led to two modern passenger jets falling out of the sky, and why the story of the fastest-selling aircraft in Boeing's history ended in tragedy. On 29 October 2018, Lion Air Flight 610 crashed into the Java Sea, and all 189 people on board died. Less than five months later, on 10 March 2019, an Ethiopian Airlines 737 MAX also crashed, and the 157 people on board were all killed. The documentary examines the crash reports to explore how these catastrophes could have been avoided, using state-of-the-art flight simulation and vivid CGI sequences to chart the flights' final moment(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dating show in which singletons meet up with possible partners at a restaurant in the hope of finding love, before deciding whether they want to take things further(n)

programme.title: Flirty Dancing
programme.desc: Although a big romcom fan, Emmerson has had his heart broken in the past and finds it hard to trust. He is matched with 28-year-old Ruby, whose dreams of becoming a professional ballet dancer were shattered when she had to have both hips replaced. Ashley Banjo creates a dance with a challenging drop that relies on trusting each other from the very first moment they meet. He also assists property manager Garry, who finds it hard to talk to men he fancies. Ashley's energetic, playful routine gives him and Ryan - who has never had a boyfriend before - the opportunity to put themselves 'out there'(n)

programme.title: Remington Steele

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team tries to solve the mystery of how three volleyball players came to die of electrocution while competing in a beach tournament. programme.title: CSI: NY

programme.title: Perception

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a clothes shop owner during an apparent robbery. An open lock suggests someone who works there was responsible, and the trail eventually leads to the son of the dead man's business partner, whose shady dealings were at risk of being exposed. However, the suspect flees to Israel before police have a chance to question him(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate a spate of murders and find a recurring pattern, leading them to believe they have a serial killer on their hands. They soon find a suspect, who admits to killing 15 women - but he then puts a disconcerting spin on things by claiming his attorney knows more about the crimes than they realise(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are called in to investigate the murder of a notorious bounty hunter. Piecing together his final movements, they turn their attentions to a meeting between the victim and an ambitious journalist, which may be able to shed light on what happened(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher answers a student's plea to look into a mysterious killing at her college - a crime to which a well-respected professor has inexplicably confessed. Guest starring EG Marshall and Ralph Waite, with Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A breakdown leaves Jessica and a fellow writer stranded at a run-down desert motel, and it is not long before the amateur sleuth encounters murder, and the gruesome events are revealed through a series of black-and-white flashbacks. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Southerlyn arrest a homeless man on suspicion of killing a fellow vagrant and gather enough evidence to bring a prosecution - but a clever defence attorney tries to get him exonerated by arguing that the laws of decent society do not apply to people who live on the streets(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a bookmaker whose testimony led to the incarceration of a Mob boss 10 years previously. The trail leads to an apparently reputable businessman with a sideline in contract killing who appears to be taking orders from the Mafia. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson(n)

programme.title: Private Eyes

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A wealthy couple are found dead at their home and it soon becomes apparent the killings are connected to a drug-smuggling ring. Crime drama, guest starring Stephanie March (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit) and Mitch Pileggi (The X Files)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Guest starring Matthew Modine (Weeds)(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: CSI: Miami

Dr Price surmises they were killed by having jump-leads attached to their bodies, and investigations eventually lead the team to a doctor who specialises in treating eye disorders - but he proves to have a cast-iron alibi and it soon becomes obvious someone is framing him(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

Crime drama, with Peter Falk and William Shatner(n)

programme.title: NCIS

Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula(n)

The Five-0 detectives soon track down the vehicle with the help of the man's autistic little brother - but the news is not good, setting them out on a murder investigation. Meanwhile, Mary arrives in Hawaii and discovers her mother is still alive, so McGarrett stages a reunion - but he doesn't bank on his sister bringing along the motor-mouthed OAP she's been caring for. Christine Lahti, Alan Ruck and Shelley Berman guest star(n)

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: The vicious murder of a college professor calls for Sherlock to apply his deductive skills to the case, while Joan receives an unexpected request for help from an ex-lover. Jonny Lee Miller stars as the modern-day version of Arthur Conan Doyle's brilliant sleuth, with Lucy Liu as his companion Dr Joan Watson(n)

programme.title: Stella
programme.desc: Rumours spread that Sunil is cheating on Emma, and Stella makes it her mission to find out the truth when Luke spots his future brother-in-law with another girl. programme.title: How to Stay Young
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which Angela Rippon and Dr Chris van Tulleken investigate the latest experiments and research that could help put the brakes on the ageing process. The first programme focuses on the physical aspects, revealing how much is down to how people live their lives and how people's lifestyle choices can help combat the effects of ageing. Chris finds out how public attitudes to the elderly can have a negative impact, spending a day as an 80-year-old with the help of Hollywood-style prosthetics, and Angela visits a study in Germany comparing the benefits of dancing with conventional gym exercises(n)

programme.title: The Dark Charisma of Adolf Hitler
programme.desc: Laurence Rees looks at the peak of the dictator's popularity in Germany throughout the 1930s and his decision to invade France in 1940. In Nuremburg, hundreds of thousands gathered to pay homage, filling the streets in celebration of a leader who had convinced them they were a superior race and would accomplish great things. During this time Hitler faced his greatest test yet as he wanted to gain a vast new empire, but had to persuade his people to maintain their faith in him and embrace war(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
